pages.content: checklist. Checklist Before Publication (pocket version) Level: intermediate · Reading time: 3 min · Prerequisite: book/poche/10-roadmap.html · Last review: 2026-05-09 TL;DR (5 lines) Read the core idea first. Understand one concept at a time. Run small examples. Fix errors early. Move to next chapter only when clear. Frequent mistakes Skipping prerequisites. Reading without trying examples. Fixing too many errors at once. Prerequisites: docs/book/poche/summary.html . See also: docs/book/chapters/checklist-avant-publication.html . Goal Understand the pre-publication checklist in a simple way to act quickly. Key idea in 3 points Identify the concrete need. Apply the correct minimum shape. Check the result with a short test. When to use it When you need an immediately readable solution. When you want to validate a base before going further. When you need to quickly explain the topic to someone else. Minimal example entry main at app/demo { return 0 } Common errors Wanting to cover all cases at once. Mix multiple changes in the same step. Forgetting to reread the exact error message. Mini-exercise (5 minutes) Edit the example to create a pre-publish checklist variation, and then verify the expected result. Quick checklist The code compiles. The result is understandable. The main limiting case is tested. If you're stuck Start from the nominal case, make a single modification, recompile, then validate the output. Go further Full version: docs/book/chapters/checklist-avant-publication.html . Mini quiz What is the main goal of this chapter? Which concept is most important? What will you try right now? Mark as reviewed See also 10. Personal Roadmap Pocket Summary Pocket Summary Next best action Apply one idea from this chapter in a tiny example, then continue.

pages.title: Stdlib Migration Plan
pages.content: Stdlib Migration Plan Goal: move from historical use std/* towards a coherent stdlib/* space, with progressive compatibility. 1) Target conventions Required module entry: mod.vit Required module metadata: info.vit Public names without _ (prefer hierarchy with / ) std/* kept only as legacy facade (re-export) 2) Existing Legacy (frozen, no new cases) Active legacy wrappers (historical path import): abi/abi.vit actor/actor.vit alerts/alerts.vit alloc/alloc.vit array/array.vit ast/ast.vit async/async.vit audio/audio.vit Legacy names with _ : circuit_breaker config_derive const_eval feature_flags http_client mock_http random_secure rate_limit schema_registry 3) Migration table old -> new http_client -> http/client mock_http -> http/mock random_secure -> random/secure schema_registry -> schema/registry rate_limit -> rate/limit circuit_breaker -> resilience/circuit_breaker feature_flags -> config/feature_flags const_eval -> compiler/const_eval config_derive -> config/derive Duplicates/clarifications: process vs subprocess : keep process public API and move the low level under process/internal (or alias subprocess -> process/spawn ). runtime vs core : core = stable primitives, runtime = bootstrap/runtime. serialize vs codec : codec = concrete encoding (json/yaml/bin), serialize = stable facade. 4) Target families core/* : types, collections, alloc, borrow system/* : fs, env, process, net, path data/* : json, yaml, sql, db, cache, stream app/* : http, ws, cli, config, jobs 5) Compatibility and migration Phase A: create the new stdlib modules + re-exports from the old ones. Phase B: depreciation warnings on legacy imports. Phase C: auto-fix ( vitte mod doctor --fix ) old -> new. Phase D: gradual removal of legacy aliases after a compat window. Plan C2 (proposed): Add vitte mod doctor --fix --write <file.vit> to apply rewrites directly to the file. Keep --fix without --write in dry-run mode (text suggestions only). Add a mode --fix --write --backup to create <file>.bak before writing. Prioritize safe and syntactic rewrites ( vitte/x/x -> vitte/x ) before any more intrusive transformation. Depreciation window: v2.x : compat active + warning E1020 on vitte/x/x . v3.0 : removal of the compat vitte/x/x (canonical import required vitte/x ). 6) CI Active rule: make package-layout-lint . This rule: refuse any new stdlib module without mod.vit / info.vit ; refuse any new name with _ ; keeps a legacy allowlist for the existing one. Allowlist files: tools/package_entry_legacy_allowlist.txt tools/package_name_legacy_allowlist.txt

pages.title: Vitte Stdlib Roadmap
pages.content: Vitte Stdlib Roadmap This document describes the target organization in Vitte Stdlib . Mandatory terminology: we are talking about stdlib (not crates). Import convention in language: stdlib name: vitte-core (documentation/architecture). path in use : vitte/core (Vitte syntax). 1) Language foundation vitte-core primitive types. error management ( Result , Option ). basic traits. abstract allocation. vitte-alloc global allocator. bump/arena. slab. fallback no_std . vitte-collections Vec . Map . Set . RingBuffer . Rope (useful for PlatonEditor). vitte-math int / float. digital traits. random. SIMD wrapper. 2) Async/Concurrency vitte-async Future . Executor . Task . Scheduler . vitte-net TCP/UDP. non-blocking sockets. Minimal HTTP. vitte-runtime complete runtime (Tokio type). reactor + scheduler. timers. io driver. 3) System & low level vitte-fs abstraction files. mmap. watchers. vitte-process spawn. job control. sandbox. vitte-abi calling conventions. register sets. lowering ABI. vitte-ffi binding C. generation header. safe wrappers. 4) Compilation / Toolchain vitte-ast . vitte-hir . vitte-mir . vitte-monomorphize . vitte-borrow . vitte-typeck . vitte-const-eval . vitte-codegen-llvm . vitte-codegen-cranelift . vitte-codegen-vitte . Structural reference: inspired by compiler_frontend , adapted to the Vitte pipeline. 5) Standard modules (std) vitte-std re-export core + alloc + collections. fs. net. io. thread. vitte-io Reader . Writer . BufReader . ANSI (type std::io + std::fmt ). 6) Dev tools vitte-test runner. expect. snapshot. vitte-bench micro bench. flamegraph export. vitte-lint Vitte style rules. custom lints (style .end , etc.). 7) Ecosystem advances vitte-serialize json. toml. bincode. derive macros. vitte-graph graph algorithms. topo comes out. dijkstra. vitte-audio wav. synth. basic dsp. vitte-gui backend abstraction. OpenGL/Vulkan wrapper. 8) Data / Scientific vitte-array ndarray N-dim. slicing. broadcasting. optional BLAS backend. vitte-stats mean / variance. linear regression. distributions. vitte-plot simple plotting. SVG/PNG export. vitte-ml perceptron. k-means. logistic regression. mini autodiff. 9) Scripting / Dynamic tools vitte-eval eval runtime. sandboxed interpreter. plugin loader. vitte-plugin dynamic modules. versioning. hot reload. vitte-shell mini shell scripting. pipelines. env tools. 10) Web advances vitte-ws WebSocket server/client. vitte-template engine templates. compile-time rendering. vitte-openapi generation spec OpenAPI. auto doc. 11) Database vitte-sql SQLite / Postgres driver. async support. vitte-migrate versioned migrations. diff schema. vitte-cache LRU. TTL. redis-like memory store. 12) Security vitte-jwt encode/decode. HMAC/RSA. vitte-tls TLS abstraction. openssl/rustls-like backend. vitte-auth session manager. role system. 13) Advanced competition vitte-channel mpsc. broadcast. bounded / unbounded. vitte-actor actor model. supervision tree. 14) Dev / Packaging vitte-pack builder artifacts. metadata. vitte-registry client registry. publish/fetch. vitte-doc HTML doc generator. search index. 15) Multimedia vitte-image PNG/JPEG. resize. color space. vitte-video ffmpeg wrapper. 16) Meta programming vitte-macro Chemin Vitte: use vitte/macros . derivative. attribute macros. vitte-reflect runtime reflection. metadata type. 17) Modern Infrastructure vitte-config-derive derive config struct. vitte-env variable management approx. vitte-scheduler cron-like tasks. 18) Batteries included (Python type) vitte-http-client Chemin Vitte: use vitte/http_client . vitte-requests Chemin Vitte: use vitte/requests . vitte-jsonpath Chemin Vitte: use vitte/jsonpath . vitte-yaml Chemin Vitte: use vitte/yaml . vitte-pickle Chemin Vitte: use vitte/pickle . vitte-datetime Chemin Vitte: use vitte/datetime . vitte-pathlib Chemin Vitte: use vitte/pathlib . vitte-subprocess Chemin Vitte: use vitte/subprocess . 19) Strategic orientation If Vitte aims for system language, priority: alloc. async. net. fs. abi. codegen. If Vitte aims for a modern backend, priority: web. db. auth. json. async runtime. If Vitte aims for tooling / IDE, priority: rope. lsp. syntax. incremental parser. If Vitte aims for data/scientific, priority: array.array. stats. plot. ml. Architectural rules vitte-core does not depend on any high-level module. vitte-alloc depends on vitte-core . vitte-collections depends on vitte-core + vitte-alloc . vitte-std re-export; the heavy logic remains in the specialized stdlib modules. vitte-runtime depends on vitte-async + vitte-net + vitte-io . vitte-ffi applies the contracts of vitte-abi . MVP priority (recommended order) vitte-core . vitte-alloc . vitte-collections . vitte-io . vitte-async . vitte-runtime . vitte-net . vitte-fs / vitte-process . vitte-std (stable re-exports).

pages.title: Stdlib (Selection)
pages.content: Stdlib (Selection) This page is a library map, not a minimal snippet dump. The goal is to show the main families, the responsibilities they own, and the kinds of complete programs they support. Library Families Core : types, strings, memory helpers, panic/runtime-adjacent basics, low-level algorithms. Collections : vector, deque, queue, stack, linked list, hashmap, hashset, graph, matrix. Data : dataset, schema, transform, merge, cleaning, statistics. Json and encoding : parse, builder, stringify, serialize, schema, utf, url, base64, hex, html. Path and io : manipulation, walkers, special locations, globbing, file, buffer, stream, stdio. Math : arithmetic, algebra, logic, geometry, trigonometry, probability, statistics, modular, matrix, vector. Crypto and compression : hashes, HMAC, random, symmetric/asymmetric primitives, huffman, lz, deflate, brotli. Async and threading : future, executor, channel, thread, mutex, threadpool. Kernel and ffi : process, scheduler, users, signals, device, memory, network, ABI-facing integration. Complete Example space demo/report form Report { name: string root: string status: int } pick Outcome { case Ok(summary: string) case Err(code: int) } proc normalize_root(root: string) -> string { if root == "" { give "." } give root } proc validate_report(r: Report) -> Outcome { if r.name == "" { give Outcome.Err(11) } if r.status < 0 { give Outcome.Err(12) } give Outcome.Ok(r.name) } proc render_report(r: Report) -> string { let normalized: string = normalize_root(r.root) give "{name=" + r.name + ",root=" + normalized + ",status=ok}" } entry main at core/app { let r: Report = Report("demo", "toolchain/stage2", 0) return 0 } This single example is enough to show where future stdlib families plug in: path for root normalization, json for serialization, io for file emission, collections for grouping reports, and async if generation becomes concurrent. How To Read The Catalog Start from the responsibility of the code, not from the module name you happen to remember. Prefer pure families first: core , strings , collections , data . Move to host-facing families only when the problem genuinely touches files, processes, or runtime coordination. Keep bridge/kernel/ffi boundaries explicit because they carry the highest coupling cost.
